title: gnaphalium luteoalbum subsp. affine
intro: Gnaphalium luteoalbum subsp. affine is a subspecies of the daisy-like flowering plant, belonging to the genus Gnaphalium, which is now classified under the genus Chrysanthemum or Pyrethrum. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of Gnaphalium luteoalbum subsp. affine include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and alleviate respiratory issues. Traditionally, the plant is used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, although further research is required to confirm its efficacy and safety for human consumption.
